everyone.name: Sara Trappetti
everyone.bio: I started my journey in Tech Marketing in 2016, and in 2021, I embarked with SIGHUP on the Cloud Native industry as a complete newbie. For the first six months, I felt overwhelmed by the vastness and complexity of the field, but then something amazing happened along the way – thanks to my stubbornness and the patient support of my colleagues, I actively started contributing to the Cloud Native community and found a perfect place to express my skills while helping others, organizing events, and supporting projects - I'm a KCD Italy and Cloud Native Milano Meetup organizer and part of the Glossary Italian Localization Group. All that apart, I'm music-passionate and a restless shower performer, a winter couch knitter and cat cuddler, a balcony gardening ace, a dabble baker, and a great backwarmer during adventurous motorcycle journeys.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Being a non-tech member and a proud CNCF ambassador, I'm determined to lend my support in any way possible. I'm here to share the lessons I've learned, contribute to the growth of our community, and empower others to discover the Cloud Native Community and find their own path. I want to be a guiding hand to help others navigate CNCF initiatives and projects. It's all about showing that there are different ways to contribute and various subgroups tailored to their expertise.

everyone.name: Arthur Berezin
everyone.bio: Arthur Berezin is a product leader and Kubernetes expert with extensive experience in cloud-native solutions. As a co-organizer of the CNCF Tel Aviv Chapter and Kubernetes Community Days Tel Aviv, Arthur is committed to fostering the DevOps and Kubernetes communities.

everyone.name: Anurag Kumar
everyone.bio: I'm contributor and maintainer of KubeArmor (A CNCF runtime security project). I'm interested in Kubernetes Security. You can read my blogs here https://kranurag.devWhy I am excited to be an ambassador: I'm excited to be a CNCF ambassador because I'll try to bring and educate more people towards CNCF ecosystem. CNCF is one of the most welcoming community. I also want to share my knowledge when it comes to Kubernetes and other CNCF projects to new peoples who're getting started. Finally, I would love to meet and connect with fellow CNCF ambassadors.

everyone.name: Sumit Kaur
everyone.bio: I am SRE/DevOps Engineer with expertise in building and operating cloud-native platforms using Kubernetes across AWS EKS, Azure AKS, Google Kubernetes Engine, and OpenShift. I actively engage with the Kubernetes and CNCF community, with a strong interest in sharing practical knowledge around platform reliability, automation, and Infrastructure as Code using tools like Terraform and Ansible. My work focuses on enabling developer productivity through scalable CI/CD pipelines and resilient Kubernetes architectures. I am passionate about mentoring, continuous learning, and contributing to the growth of the cloud-native ecosystem by automating and simplifying complex operational challenges.

everyone.name: Anton Krasnobaev
everyone.bio: I am a Senior DevOps Engineer with over 10 years of experience in IT, specializing in cloud infrastructure, automation, and container orchestration. I’m passionate about Kubernetes and the CNCF ecosystem, where I focus on building scalable, secure, and maintainable systems. A strong advocate for Infrastructure as Code and CI/CD best practices, I enjoy turning complex challenges into elegant, automated solutions. I’m an active constantly explore new tools to push the boundaries of modern DevOps.

everyone.name: Dulmandakh Sukhbaatar
everyone.bio: I'm a Cloud Native architect and DevSecOps consultant with over 20 years of experience in designing, implementing, and securing IT systems.

ambassadors.name: Sara Trappetti
ambassadors.bio: I started my journey in Tech Marketing in 2016, and in 2021, I embarked with SIGHUP on the Cloud Native industry as a complete newbie. For the first six months, I felt overwhelmed by the vastness and complexity of the field, but then something amazing happened along the way – thanks to my stubbornness and the patient support of my colleagues, I actively started contributing to the Cloud Native community and found a perfect place to express my skills while helping others, organizing events, and supporting projects - I'm a KCD Italy and Cloud Native Milano Meetup organizer and part of the Glossary Italian Localization Group. All that apart, I'm music-passionate and a restless shower performer, a winter couch knitter and cat cuddler, a balcony gardening ace, a dabble baker, and a great backwarmer during adventurous motorcycle journeys.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Being a non-tech member and a proud CNCF ambassador, I'm determined to lend my support in any way possible. I'm here to share the lessons I've learned, contribute to the growth of our community, and empower others to discover the Cloud Native Community and find their own path. I want to be a guiding hand to help others navigate CNCF initiatives and projects. It's all about showing that there are different ways to contribute and various subgroups tailored to their expertise.

ambassadors.name: Chenghao Yang
ambassadors.bio: ChengHao is a co-organizer of the Cloud Native Taiwan User Group (CNTUG), assisting in organizing monthly meetups and guiding new community members to contribute. He is interested in exploring each CNCF project and regularly helps resolve issues. Occasionally, he writes technical articles on Kubernetes/CNCF on his blog. Feel free to visit!
ambassadors.company: Wizigo Inc. (SRE engineer) / Tzu Chi University (Graduate Student)

ambassadors.name: Tal Yitzhak
ambassadors.bio: I'm a passionate DevOps Engineer and Solutions Architect with expertise in cloud-native technologies and a strong focus on CNCF projects like ArgoCD, Argo Rollouts, OpenTelemetry, and Jaeger. Through my blog posts on Medium, active participation in the CNCF community, and contributions to various projects such as ArgoCD and Argo Rollouts, I aim to promote and educate others about the benefits of cloud-native technologies. As a CNCF member, I continue to support the community through my work with CNCF Projects i'm passionate about like Argo, OpenTelemetry and Jaeger. Connect with me on Medium (https://medium.com/@talyitzhak) to explore real-world production use cases and insights into CNCF technologies.

ambassadors.name: Gerrit Tamboer
ambassadors.bio: As a Platform Engineer with over 15 years of experience, I specialize in Cloud Native technologies and Kubernetes, focusing on driving these innovations to the edge. In 2016, I founded Fullstaq, a consulting company dedicated to advancing Cloud Native solutions, and through its merger into TrueFullstaq, I now serve as Chief Evangelist. I’m passionate about sharing insights and best practices in the CNCF ecosystem, actively engaging in the community to promote Kubernetes and edge computing.

ambassadors.name: Sunyanan Choochotkaew
ambassadors.bio: I’m a researcher at IBM Research – Tokyo, specializing in distributed computing and performance acceleration on cloud platforms. My CNCF journey began as a Kepler maintainer, and I now contribute to Kubernetes and its SIGs. I’m passionate about networking, optimization in autoscaling and scheduling, and observability. I actively engage in community groups to advance cloud-native technologies, and I’m happy to share my knowledge and help new contributors onboard—contributing to the cloud-native community.

ambassadors.name: Seungkyu Ahn
ambassadors.bio: Seungkyu Ahn is a senior IT leader with 30+ years of expertise in AI, Cloud Native, and Enterprise architectureKubernetes & AI Infrastructure: Extensive experience building Kubernetes platforms for finance and telco. Currently researching secure AI serving via GPUs and network optimization using eBPF.Cloud & OpenStack: Led the design and operation of global Private Cloud data centers across Korea, China, and the US, resolving complex infrastructure challenges in storage and networking.Enterprise Leadership: Expert in architecting and operating large-scale systems under high demand. Led multidisciplinary teams of up to 100 members through year-long projects, including critical version upgrades and system stabilizations.Why I am excited to be an ambassador:I am deeply passionate about advancing the adoption and understanding of cloud-native technologies, particularly within the Korean technical community. Throughout my professional journey, I have been dedicated to open source advocacy, technical education, and community leadership, with a focus on bridging the gap between global cloud-native advancements and local practitioners. Notably, I have taken a leading role in organizing KCD Seoul and have served as an organizer for the Cloud Native Community Groups (Seoul), effectively acting as an ambassador for CNCF within Korea. Through these activities, I have empowered Korean developers and organizations to fully leverage CNCF projects by providing timely, accessible, and high-quality technical content, workshops, and mentorship programs. I am committed to reducing language and cultural barriers by localizing key resources, fostering inclusive community spaces, and encouraging active participation in global CNCF initiatives. As a CNCF Ambassador, I aim to be a trusted liaison, ensuring that the Korean community not only keeps pace with global trends but also contributes meaningfully to the evolution of the cloud-native ecosystem.

ambassadors.name: Bret Fisher
ambassadors.bio: For 25 years, I've built and operated distributed systems and helped over a million people learn dev and ops topics. I'm also a Docker Captain and the creator of the wildly popular Docker & Kubernetes Mastery series on Udemy. I provide DevOps-style consulting, workshops, a popular DevOps Discord server, a container-focused YouTube channel, and a podcast. I basically spend my days helping people and giving high fives.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I think DevOps culture and container tech have changed the IT Pro and Developer world forever! For over eight years, I've been a megaphone for container tech and love teaching cloud native DevOps topics. The CNCF community has been a great experience for me both as a teacher and a learner, and I want to make sure it's great for many years to come.

ambassadors.name: Ali Ok
ambassadors.bio: Ali is a Principal Software Engineer at Red Hat, where he focuses on developing and contributing to open-source software. He has a strong track record of contributions to various projects, communities, and foundations, and he is currently an active contributor to the Knative project. In addition to his technical contributions, Ali is also deeply committed to mentoring and promoting contributor growth within the CNCF community. He plays an active role in the Google Summer of Code program at CNCF, where he serves as an admin. He is also a frequent speaker on topics such as starting to contribute to open-source software and the projects he is working on.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Becoming a CNCF Ambassador is an exciting opportunity for me to expand my reach and impact within the community. As someone who is already involved in mentoring and contributor growth activities at CNCF, I'm eager to use the ambassador title to promote CNCF technologies, open-source software, and help interested individuals get started with contributing to CNCF projects. Additionally, being part of a network of other CNCF Ambassadors will allow me to collaborate and share ideas with like-minded individuals who are passionate about the same things. I'm excited to be part of this community and look forward to making a meaningful impact.

kubestronauts.name: Sumit Kaur
kubestronauts.bio: I am SRE/DevOps Engineer with expertise in building and operating cloud-native platforms using Kubernetes across AWS EKS, Azure AKS, Google Kubernetes Engine, and OpenShift. I actively engage with the Kubernetes and CNCF community, with a strong interest in sharing practical knowledge around platform reliability, automation, and Infrastructure as Code using tools like Terraform and Ansible. My work focuses on enabling developer productivity through scalable CI/CD pipelines and resilient Kubernetes architectures. I am passionate about mentoring, continuous learning, and contributing to the growth of the cloud-native ecosystem by automating and simplifying complex operational challenges.

kubestronauts.name: Carmelo San Giovanni
kubestronauts.bio: As a Staff Engineer at Security Compass and Kubestronaut, I use Kubernetes to automate away operational toil, drive the adoption of best-in-class tools, and streamline the developer experience, ultimately creating platforms that accelerate release cycles and improve our security posture. My focus is on building infrastructure so seamless it fades into the background, allowing developers to innovate freely while trusting in resilient, self-healing systems.  I believe the pursuit of mastery demands constant adaptability, and I actively share that journey by mentoring my team on emerging cloud-native technologies.

kubestronauts.name: Sajeeva Nambadawa Vithanage
kubestronauts.bio: A security-conscious technology fanatic with over 12 years of experience, I am deeply passionate about cloud-native designs and architecture. In addition to my Kubernetes certifications, I hold several other certifications, including Istio Certified Associate, Prometheus Certified Associate, Associate Cloud Engineer (GCP), Professional Cloud Database Engineer, AWS Certified Solutions Architect - Associate, Terraform Associate, 5 X Oracle, RHCE, CCNA, and Certified Ethical Hacker.

kubestronauts.name: Joshua Taylor
kubestronauts.bio: Experienced Devops Engineer working with Kubernetes, Infrastructure as Code, CI/CD, and various cloud deployments throughout AWS, GCP, & Azure. I enjoy working on projects in Go and Rust. Outside of work, I love learning C++ and C# for video game development in Unreal Engine and Unity.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Jana Vonšák
goldenKubestronauts.bio: DevOps Security Engineer fascinated by the tech world 🤖. Fueled by coffee ☕. Hitting the gym in my free time 💪🏻. Here to grab a fancy jacket 🌞

goldenKubestronauts.name: Pedro Célestin
goldenKubestronauts.bio: I’m a seasoned IT professional with over a decade of experience in mission-critical environments. I excel in designing, deploying and sustaining dependable infrastructure solutions, drawn upon cloud-native technologies such as Kubernetes, Prometheus, Thanos, Istio and Argo. I always seek to keep systems architecture tightly aligned with business goals, and for such I make wide use of service level objectives crafted upon observability data. I strongly believe that great architectures enable great digital products that impact peoples lives.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Min Khant Zaw
goldenKubestronauts.bio: Open Source Enthusiast and experienced Linux System Administrator specialized in open-source technologies, dedicated to delivering efficient and secure services to the finance and telecom sectors. Focusing on architecting resilient microservices across multiple cloud providers using Infrastructure as Code (IaC) and leveraging CNCF's open-source technologies.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Sahil Anand
goldenKubestronauts.bio: I am a seasoned Kubernetes specialist and an avid IT and cloud enthusiast. With a wealth of expertise spanning Kubernetes, Cloud, Networking, DevOps, Monitoring, design and architecture, I have established myself as a versatile professional in the tech industry.  My proficiency is not limited to hands-on technical skills; I have also excelled and love to impart knowledge. I have keen interest and have delivered numerous technical trainings on a wide array of topics such as Cloud, Kubernetes, DevOps, Networking, and Troubleshooting, etc. helping many professionals and organizations advance their technological capabilities.
